identity.name: AI Access — AWS Access Key Leaked to Anthropic API
identity.summary: A user pastes a debugging snippet containing live-looking AWS credentials into Anthropic Claude. The llm_provider_egress plugin POSTs a secret payload carrying an AKIA-prefixed access key (AKIA0000CORTEXSIMCANARY) and a DB connection string to api.anthropic.com/v1/messages; Cortex AI Access Security DLP matches the AWS-key regex AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} in the prompt body and a block-action policy fires.
identity.description: Cloud-credential disclosure to external LLMs is a high-severity insider data-loss path: a developer pastes a failing boto3 script — keys and all — into a public model to debug it, leaking long-lived AWS credentials over TLS. This scenario emits an authentic-shape POST to api.anthropic.com/v1/messages whose prompt body contains an AKIA-prefixed access key, an AWS secret-access-key string and a Postgres connection URI, each carrying the CORTEXSIM-CANARY marker. Detection is layered: (1) an IOC/DLP regex AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} matches the access key in the decrypted prompt body; (2) an analytics rule flags the POST to a non-sanctioned provider from this user context; (3) the NGFW generative-AI App-ID classifies the egress; (4) a block-action policy denies subsequent secret-bearing sessions after the first DLP hit. A correlation rule fuses the regex hit with the App-ID match into one critical Credential-Disclosure-to-LLM incident.

prerequisites.description: Cortex AI Access Security licensed and inline with secrets DLP profile enabled.
Lab environment matching scenario SIM-AIACC-002 requirements (egress allowed to api.anthropic.com).

payload.code:
# step-1: Pre-flight: confirm secret payload contains the canary AKIA key
python3 -c "
from core.eal_simulator.plugins.llm_provider_egress import _render_payload
assert 'AKIA0000CORTEXSIMCANARY' in _render_payload('secret')
print('canary marker present in secret payload')
"
# step-2: Single secret POST to api.anthropic.com via EAL plugin
cat > /tmp/aiacc-002.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-AIACC-002
name: "AI Access — AWS key leak to Anthropic"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - api.anthropic.com
dry_run: false
tags: [ai-access, anthropic, secret-dlp]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: llm_provider_egress
    params:
      provider: anthropic
      payload_type: secret
      iterations: 1
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/aiacc-002.yml --live
# step-3: Verify per-session block-action policy (4 attempts)
cat > /tmp/aiacc-002-burst.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-AIACC-002
name: "AI Access — secret burst"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - api.anthropic.com
dry_run: false
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: llm_provider_egress
    params:
      provider: anthropic
      payload_type: secret
      iterations: 4
      sleep_seconds: 3
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/aiacc-002-burst.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Prompt body to api.anthropic.com contains an AKIA-prefixed AWS access key.
Outbound HTTPS POST to api.anthropic.com on the messages path.

iocs.context: AI Access — DLP regex match: AKIA-prefixed AWS access key in outbound LLM prompt. The canary access key AKIA0000CORTEXSIMCANARY matches the AWS access-key pattern AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} inside a prompt body POSTed to api.anthropic.com. In a real environment this slot is the live leaked key; the DLP regex match on the decrypted prompt body is the IOC.

biocs.name: AIACC-002 Pre-flight Secret Payload Contains AKIA Canary
biocs.description: Pre-flight: secret payload contains AKIA-prefixed canary. Confirms the secret payload renders the AKIA0000CORTEXSIMCANARY marker on the worker before any egress; matches the python self-check process invocation.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("python", "python3")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "_render_payload"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "AKIA0000CORTEXSIMCANARY"

xql_queries.name: AIACC-002 POST to Anthropic From Non-Sanctioned User Context
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "api.anthropic.com" or tls_sni = "api.anthropic.com"
| filter http_method = "POST" and uri_path contains "/v1/messages"
| filter request_body ~= "AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}"
| fields _time, source_user, src_ip, dst_host, uri_path, bytes_sent, app

xql_queries.name: AIACC-002 NGFW Generative AI App-ID Outbound HTTPS to Anthropic
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "api.anthropic.com" or tls_sni = "api.anthropic.com"
| filter app in ("anthropic-api", "claude", "generative-ai", "ai-ml-application")
| fields _time, source_user, src_ip, dst_host, app, app_category, bytes_sent, action

xql_queries.name: AIACC-002 Repeated Secret Egress to Anthropic Block Policy Trigger
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "api.anthropic.com"
| filter http_method = "POST" and uri_path contains "/v1/messages"
| filter request_body ~= "AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}"
| comp count() as attempts, count_distinct(action) as action_kinds by source_user, src_ip
| filter attempts >= 2
| fields source_user, src_ip, attempts, action_kinds

correlation_rules.name: AWS Credential Disclosure to Anthropic LLM
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(AIACC-002 Pre-flight Secret Payload Contains AKIA Canary) OR XQL(AIACC-002 POST to Anthropic From Non-Sanctioned User Context) OR XQL(AIACC-002 NGFW Generative AI App-ID Outbound HTTPS to Anthropic) OR XQL(AIACC-002 Repeated Secret Egress to Anthropic Block Policy Trigger)

analytics_modules: Cortex AI Access Security — Secrets DLP regex (AWS access keys)
Cortex AI Access Security — Sanctioned-vs-unsanctioned provider policy

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter dst_host = "api.anthropic.com" and request_body ~= "AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}" | fields _time, source_user, dst_host, bytes_sent, action
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter app in ("anthropic-api","claude","generative-ai") | fields _time, source_user, dst_host, app, action
Correlation CR-AIACC-0002 fuses the AKIA regex DLP hit with the generative-AI App-ID match into one critical credential-disclosure incident.

use_cases.name: Cloud Credential Disclosure to External LLM
use_cases.description: Detect AWS access keys pasted into a public LLM provider and enforce a block-action policy after the first DLP hit.

test_cases.objective: A secret POST to api.anthropic.com fires the AKIA AWS-key DLP regex.
preconditions: Cortex AI Access Security inline with TLS decrypt and secrets DLP profile.
AWS access-key DLP pattern enabled in Block mode.
steps: Observe the secrets DLP block alert in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: IOC `aws-access-key: AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}` matches the prompt body.
XQL `AIACC-002 POST to Anthropic From Non-Sanctioned User Context` returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Repeated secret-bearing sessions are denied by the block-action policy and stitch into one incident.
success_criteria: XQL `AIACC-002 Repeated Secret Egress to Anthropic Block Policy Trigger` returns >= 1 row.
Correlation `CR-AIACC-0002` groups the signals into one critical incident.

preventive_controls: Enable Cortex AI Access Security secrets DLP (AWS access keys) in Block mode for all generative-AI destinations.
Rotate and short-TTL all AWS credentials; replace static keys with IAM roles / IRSA so leaked keys are useless.
Block direct egress to public LLM provider APIs and route through a sanctioned, DLP-inspected gateway.
detection_engineering: Match the AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} regex (and provider secret patterns) in decrypted LLM prompt bodies.
Flag any secret-bearing POST to a non-sanctioned provider from a corporate user context.
Alert when a block-action policy denies repeated secret egress from the same user.

references.title: Unit42 - Cloud Credentials in LLM Prompts
MITRE ATT&CK T1552.001 — Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files
